title: using(allocator) erased from function pointer types
description: A function pointer type 'fn([]const u8) \!Value' can refer to a using(allocator) function. The pointer type carries no annotation of the allocation requirement. A caller storing a function pointer and calling it outside a 'with' block will compile but panic or silently use the wrong allocator at runtime. The allocation requirement is invisible at the call site through the pointer. What caller observes: runtime panic or UB, no compile error.
design: Fat pointers. All function pointers in Zinc are two words: { fn_ptr: *const fn(...), ctx: *anyopaque }. When taking a pointer to a using(X) function inside a with(X: ...) block, the compiler captures X into the context slot. At the indirect call site, the context is threaded back in. When taking a pointer to a non-using function, ctx is null and the call is a direct dispatch. The function pointer type spelling is unchanged — *const fn([]const u8) !Value covers both cases. No two spellings. Cost: all function pointers double in size (one extra word). Same representation Zig already uses for std.mem.Allocator.
